versions.deck: The Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) has banned Sri Lanka’s T20I captain and all-rounder Dasun Shanaka from the Pakistan Super League (PSL) for one season.

clusters.title: World Bank sees Sri Lanka growth slowing to 3.6% in 2026 amid energy shock

versions.title: World Bank sees Sri Lanka growth slowing to 3.6% in 2026 amid energy shock
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s economic growth is expected to slow to 3.6% in 2026 as higher energy prices weigh on activity, although the economy will regain its pre-crisis size last seen in 2018, the World Bank said in its latest South Asia Economic Update April 2026.

clusters.title: Sri Lanka better placed to cushion energy shock, needs targeted support: IMF

versions.title: Sri Lanka better placed to cushion energy shock, needs targeted support: IMF
versions.deck: Sri Lanka is in a stronger position to cushion the impact of rising energy costs following improvements in revenue mobilisation, though vulnerabilities remain due to its reliance on fuel imports, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) said last week.

clusters.title: Philip Gunawardena and his positive mindset: Speeches in the last lap of Parliament

versions.title: Philip Gunawardena and his positive mindset: Speeches in the last lap of Parliament
versions.deck: The Philip Gunawardena Commemorative Society has released Volume III of the speeches of Philip Gunawardena in Parliament from 1965 to 1970. Previously, the Commemorative Society had issued a compilation of his speeches in the State Council during the colonial period as Volume I, and those in Parliament in independent Sri Lanka as Volume II.

clusters.title: Seven deadly sins of Sri Lanka’s dastardly 2019 Easter bombings

versions.title: Seven deadly sins of Sri Lanka’s dastardly 2019 Easter bombings
versions.deck: Seven years on from that black Easter Sunday of 2019, the toll of grief has not let up its death grip. Nor have the traumatic memories of the dark day which unshrouded terror diminished. The wound has merely deepened into something more corrosive and even cancerous – since it has been left untreated.

clusters.title: End of corporate shadows: Mandatory Beneficial Ownership disclosure begins

versions.title: End of corporate shadows: Mandatory Beneficial Ownership disclosure begins
versions.deck: Sri Lanka is witnessing a landmark change in its corporate governance as the new beneficial ownership rules take effect. For decades, the true individuals pulling the strings of complex corporate structures could remain hidden behind layers of holding companies and nominee shareholders. That era of anonymity officially ends on 30 March 2026, as the Companies (Amendment) Act, No. 12 of 2025 and the Companies (Beneficial Ownership) Regulations, No

clusters.title: Sri Lanka’s banking sector: When narrative strength outpaces operational truth

versions.title: Sri Lanka’s banking sector: When narrative strength outpaces operational truth
versions.deck: The recent escalation of fraud exposure at National Development Bank PLC — from Rs. 380 million to Rs. 13.2 billion within days — has rightly drawn regulatory and public attention.

clusters.title: When systems fail: Data breaches, financial crime, and illusion of regulation

versions.title: When systems fail: Data breaches, financial crime, and illusion of regulation
versions.deck: Recent developments only reinforce these concerns. The reported Rs. 13.2 billion fraud at the National Development Bank PLC and the summoning of its senior officials to the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) is not an isolated incident, it is a symptom of a deeper systemic weakness. During this time, certain patterns became increasingly clear.

clusters.title: Conscience of Minister Kumara Jayakody and political leadership

versions.title: Conscience of Minister Kumara Jayakody and political leadership
versions.deck: The Government led by Anura Kumara Dissanayake came to power on a clear and uncompromising promise: to eradicate fraud and corruption that had long functioned as a structural cancer within Sri Lanka’s political economy.

clusters.title: Global cost of Iran crisis: United Nations must ensure people don’t pay the price of far-away conflicts

versions.title: Global cost of Iran crisis: United Nations must ensure people don’t pay the price of far-away conflicts
versions.deck: We live in an era where distance has collapsed: a natural disaster, IT breach or regional war can impact supply chains, financial markets, and migration routes, reaching every society. In our interconnected world, injustice travels as fast as risk. International solidarity and cooperation must travel faster.

clusters.title: Disruption to stability: Why must Sri Lankan exporters turn to Japan now?

versions.title: Disruption to stability: Why must Sri Lankan exporters turn to Japan now?
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s export sector is facing an uncomfortable reality. Markets that once offered predictability—the Middle East, the European Union, the United Kingdom, and the United States—are no longer as stable as they once seemed.

clusters.title: Pathiratne (5/41) rattles defending champions Galle

versions.title: Pathiratne (5/41) rattles defending champions Galle
versions.deck: Last year’s runner-up Dambulla did well to dismiss defending champions Galle for 255 and put themselves in a position to gain the vital first innings lead on the opening day of their National Super League (NSL) 4-day match at the R. Premadasa International Cricket Stadium yesterday.

clusters.title: Real Sociedad upset Atletico Madrid to clinch fourth Copa del Rey title

versions.title: Real Sociedad upset Atletico Madrid to clinch fourth Copa del Rey title
versions.deck: Real Sociedad beat Atletico Madrid 4-3 on penalties to win the Copa del Rey on Saturday following a 2-2 draw after extra time, with goalkeeper Unai Marrero saving two spot kicks in the shootout to help his side win the trophy for the fourth time.

clusters.title: Humanoid robot breaks half marathon world record in Beijing

versions.title: Humanoid robot breaks half marathon world record in Beijing
versions.deck: A humanoid robot competing against flesh-and-blood runners has broken the world record at a Beijing half-marathon, showcasing the rapid technological advancements achieved by Chinese makers.

clusters.title: New Zealand ‘A’ finish on top in tame draw at Hambantota

versions.title: New Zealand ‘A’ finish on top in tame draw at Hambantota
versions.deck: New Zealand ‘A’ who lost the 3-match unofficial ODI series 3-0 to Sri Lanka ‘A’ can be quite content with their performance in the first 4-day unofficial test which ended in a tame draw at the Mahinda Rajapaksa Stadium, Hambantota yesterday.

clusters.title: Sri Lanka U19 Women emerge Tri-Series champions

versions.title: Sri Lanka U19 Women emerge Tri-Series champions
versions.deck: Sri Lanka U19 Women’s cricket team led by Chamodi Praboda emerged champions of the Under19 Women’s Tri-Series in Australia featuring unofficial WODI and T20I matches.
